data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using stdio transport with:
1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

Let me create a complete working project for them. I'll need to:
1. Set up a C# project with the MCP SDK NuGet package
2. Create the MCP server with stdio transport
3. Add a tool that uses HttpClient
4. Add a prompt template for summarization
5. Configure logging to use stderr (not stdout) to avoid interfering with JSON-RPC


6. First, I'll check what's available in the current directory to understand the setup.

1. # MCP C# SDK
2. 
3. [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)

5. The official C# SDK for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), enabling .NET applications, services, and libraries to implement and interact with MCP clients and servers. Please visit the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.
6. 
7. ## Packages
8. 
9. This SDK consists of three main packages:
10.

12. 
13. - **[ModelContextProtocol](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol) - The main package with hosting and dependency injection extensions. References `ModelContextProtocol`.
16.

18.

20.

22. 
23. ## About MCP
24.

 # MCP C# SDK
 
 [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)
 
 The official C# SDK for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), enabling .NET applications, services, and libraries to implement and interact with MCP clients and servers. Please visit the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.
 
 ## Packages
 
 This SDK consists of three main packages:
 
 - **[ModelContextProtocol.Core](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.Core.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core) - For projects that only need to use the client or low-level server APIs and want the minimum number of dependencies.
 
 - **[ModelContextProtocol](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol) - The main package with hosting and dependency injection extensions. References `ModelContextProtocol.Core`. This is the right fit for most projects that don't need HTTP server capabilities.
 
 - **[ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore) - The library for HTTP-based MCP servers. References `ModelContextProtocol`.
 
 ## Getting Started
 
 To get started, see the [Getting Started](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/concepts/getting-started.html) guide for installation instructions, package-selection guidance, and complete examples for both clients and servers.
 
 You can also browse the [samples](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/csharp-sdk/tree/main/samples) directory and the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.
 
 ## About MCP
 
 The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to Large Language Models (LLMs). It enables secure integration between LLMs and various data sources and tools.
 
 For more information about MCP:
 
 - [Official MCP Documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)
 - [MCP C# SDK Documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/)
 - [Protocol Specification](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/)
 - [GitHub Organization](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol)

            <summary>
            Adds a server transport that uses standard input (stdin) and standard output (stdout) for communication.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This method configures the server to communicate using the standard input and output streams,
            which is commonly used when the Model Context Protocol server is launched locally by a client process.
            </para>
            <para>
            When using this transport, the server runs as a single-session service that exits when the
            stdin stream is closed. This makes it suitable for scenarios where the server should terminate
            when the parent process disconnects.

            <summary>
            Indicates that a method should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as tools in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods become available as tools that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
            When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
            </para>

            of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s
            <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.ProtocolTool"/>'s <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.InputSchema"/>. Those parameters are deserialized from the
            <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
            <list type="bullet">

                <description>
                  <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the

            <summary>
            Adds types marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute"/> attribute from the given assembly as tools to the server.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="serializerOptions">The serializer options governing tool parameter marshalling.</param>

            <remarks>
            <para>
            This method scans the specified assembly (or the calling assembly if none is provided) for classes
            marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute"/>. It then discovers all methods within those
            classes that are marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/> and registers them as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>s
            in the <paramref name="builder"/>'s <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/>.
            </para>

            The method automatically handles both static and instance methods. For instance methods, a new instance
            of the containing class is constructed for each invocation of the tool.

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from collections import defaultdict

    if 'WithTools' in name or 'WithPrompts' in name:
        base_name = name.split('(')[0] if '(' in name else name
        overloads[base_name].append(name)

for method_name in sorted(overloads.keys()):
    print(f"\n{method_name.split('.')[-1]}:")
    for sig in overloads[method_name]:
        # Extract parameters
        if '(' in sig:
            params = sig[sig.index('(')+1:sig.rindex(')')]
            print(f"  - ({params})")
EOF

            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as tools in the Model Context Protocol. When a class

            these methods become available as tools that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
            When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
            </para>
            <para>
            By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
            of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s
            <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.ProtocolTool"/>'s <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.InputSchema"/>. Those parameters are deserialized from the
            <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
            <list type="bullet">
              <item>
                <description>
                  <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
                  <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's

<member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute">
            <summary>
            Indicates that a method should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as tools in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods become available as tools that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
            When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
            </para>
            <para>
            By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
            of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s
            <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.ProtocolTool"/>'s <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.InputSchema"/>. Those parameters are deserialized from the
            <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
            <list type="bullet">
              <item>
                <description>
                  <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
                  <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
                  <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>. The parameter is not included in the generated JSON schema.
                </description>
              </item>
              <item>
                <description>
                  <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for this request,
                  and are not included in the JSON schema.
                </description>
              </item>
              <item>
                <description>
                  <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> parameters are not included in the JSON schema and are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>
                  instance associated with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
                  what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
                </description>
              </item>
              <item>
                <description>
                  <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
                  are not included in the JSON schema and are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured
                  to forward progress notifications from the tool to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request,
                  progress reports issued to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
                  that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
                </description>
              </item>
              <item>
                <description>
                  When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via
                  <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
                  according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will not be included in the generated JSON schema and will be resolved
                  from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument collection.
                </description>
              </item>
              <item>
                <description>
                  Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the
                  <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument
                  collection, and will not be included in the generated JSON schema.
                </description>
              </item>
            </list>
            </para>
            <para>
            All other parameters are deserialized from the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>s in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary,
            using the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> supplied in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.SerializerOptions"/>, or if none was provided,
            using <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            In general, the data supplied via the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/>'s dictionary is passed along from the caller and
            should thus be considered unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of the tool, consider having
            the tool be an instance method, referring to data stored in the instance, or using an instance or parameters resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
            to provide data to the method.
            </para>
            <para>
            The tool method is responsible for validating its own input arguments (e.g., checking required fields, value ranges, string lengths, or
            any other business rules). Data annotations such as <c>RequiredAttribute</c> and
            <c>MaxLengthAttribute</c> on parameter types influence the generated JSON schema exposed
            to clients, but they are not enforced at runtime by the SDK. Validation should be performed explicitly within the tool method.
<exited with exit code 0>

            <summary>

            When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerResource.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
            </para>
            <para>
            Read resource requests do not contain separate arguments, only a URI. However, for templated resources, portions of that URI may be considered
            as arguments and may be bound to parameters. Further, resource methods may accept parameters that will be bound to arguments based on their type.

# MCP C# SDK

[![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)

The official C# SDK for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), enabling .NET applications, services, and libraries to implement and interact with MCP clients and servers. Please visit the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.

## Packages

This SDK consists of three main packages:

- **[ModelContextProtocol.Core](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.Core.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core) - For projects that only need to use the client or low-level server APIs and want the minimum number of dependencies.

- **[ModelContextProtocol](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol) - The main package with hosting and dependency injection extensions. References `ModelContextProtocol.Core`. This is the right fit for most projects that don't need HTTP server capabilities.

- **[ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore) - The library for HTTP-based MCP servers. References `ModelContextProtocol`.

## Getting Started

To get started, see the [Getting Started](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/concepts/getting-started.html) guide for installation instructions, package-selection guidance, and complete examples for both clients and servers.

You can also browse the [samples](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/csharp-sdk/tree/main/samples) directory and the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.

## About MCP

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to Large Language Models (LLMs). It enables secure integration between LLMs and various data sources and tools.

For more information about MCP:

- [Official MCP Documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)
- [MCP C# SDK Documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/)
- [Protocol Specification](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/)
- [GitHub Organization](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol)
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content: Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary by checking one more important piece - the IHostBuilder extensions:

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

# Check the main package XML for builder integration
xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

# Find extension methods on IServiceCollection
extension_methods = []
for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    if 'McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions' in name or 'McpServerBuilderExtensions' in name:
        if '(' in name:
            base_method = name.split('(')[0].split('.')[-1]
            extension_methods.append((name, base_method))

# Group by method name
from collections import defaultdict
by_method = defaultdict(list)
for full_name, method in extension_methods:
    by_method[method].append(full_name)

print("Key extension methods in McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions and McpServerBuilderExtensions:")
for method_name in sorted(set(m for _, m in extension_methods)):
    print(f"\n{method_name}:")
    for full_name in by_method[method_name]:
        params = full_name[full_name.index('(')+1:full_name.rindex(')')]
        if len(params) > 100:
            params = params[:100] + "..."
        print(f"  - {params}")
EOF

## ModelContextProtocol 1.2.0 C# API Shape - Complete Analysis

### 1. **Registering an MCP Server with Stdio Transport**

**Entry Point:** Use `IServiceCollection.AddMcpServer()` from the **Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection** namespace

```csharp
// With HostBuilder/HostApplicationBuilder
var builder = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args);
builder.Services.AddMcpServer(options =>
{
    options.ServerInfo = new() { Name = "MyServer", Version = "1.0.0" };
    options.ServerInstructions = "Server instructions";
})
.WithStdioServerTransport()
.WithTools<MyToolsClass>()
.WithPrompts<MyPromptsClass>();
```

**Method signature:**
- `AddMcpServer(IServiceCollection, Action<McpServerOptions>)` → returns `IMcpServerBuilder`

**Transport extension method:**
- `WithStdioServerTransport(IMcpServerBuilder)` - Configures stdio (stdin/stdout) communication, typically used for local client-spawned servers that exit when stdin closes

**Alternative Transports:**
- `WithStreamServerTransport(IMcpServerBuilder, Stream input, Stream output)`
- For HTTP: `WithHttpTransport()` (in AspNetCore package)

---

### 2. **Defining Tools - Attribute-Based Pattern**

Tools use **attribute-based registration** with two levels of attributes:

**Class-level attribute:**
```csharp
[McpServerToolType]  // Marks the class as containing tools
public static class MyTools
{
    [McpServerTool]  // Marks individual methods as tools
    [Description("Tool description")]
    public static string MyTool(
        [Description("Parameter description")] string param1,
        int param2)
    {
        return "result";
    }
}
```

**Attributes:**
- `[McpServerToolType]` - Marks a type as containing tool methods (used with `WithToolsFromAssembly`)
- `[McpServerTool]` - Marks a method as a tool to be exposed to clients

**Registration methods** (on `IMcpServerBuilder`):
- `WithTools<TToolType>(JsonSerializerOptions)` - Generic: discovers `[McpServerTool]` methods on type `TToolType`
- `WithTools<TToolType>(TToolType target, JsonSerializerOptions)` - Uses a provided instance as the target for instance methods
- `WithTools(IEnumerable<McpServerTool>)` - Register pre-built `McpServerTool` instances directly
- `WithTools(IEnumerable<Type>, JsonSerializerOptions)` - Multiple types with tool methods
- `WithToolsFromAssembly(Assembly, JsonSerializerOptions)` - Auto-discovers all `[McpServerToolType]` classes in an assembly

---

### 3. **Defining Prompt Templates - Attribute-Based Pattern**

Prompts use the **same attribute-based pattern** as tools:

```csharp
[McpServerPromptType]  // Marks the class as containing prompts
public static class MyPrompts
{
    [McpServerPrompt]  // Marks individual methods as prompts
    [Description("Prompt description")]
    public static string MyPrompt(string input)
    {
        return "prompt result";
    }
}
```

**Attributes:**
- `[McpServerPromptType]` - Marks a type as containing prompt methods (used with `WithPromptsFromAssembly`)
- `[McpServerPrompt]` - Marks a method as a prompt to be exposed to clients

**Registration methods** (on `IMcpServerBuilder`):
- `WithPrompts<TPromptType>(JsonSerializerOptions)` - Generic: discovers `[McpServerPrompt]` methods
- `WithPrompts<TPromptType>(TPromptType target, JsonSerializerOptions)` - Uses provided instance
- `WithPrompts(IEnumerable<McpServerPrompt>)` - Pre-built prompt instances
- `WithPrompts(IEnumerable<Type>, JsonSerializerOptions)` - Multiple types
- `WithPromptsFromAssembly(Assembly, JsonSerializerOptions)` - Auto-discovers all `[McpServerPromptType]` classes

---

### 4. **Attributes and Interfaces**

**For Tools:**
- `McpServerToolAttribute` - Method-level attribute
- `McpServerToolTypeAttribute` - Class-level attribute
- `McpServerTool` - Abstract base class representing an MCP tool
- `AIFunctionMcpServerTool` - Concrete implementation wrapping `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction`
- `DelegatingMcpServerTool` - Base class for wrapping/decorating tools

**For Prompts:**
- `McpServerPromptAttribute` - Method-level attribute
- `McpServerPromptTypeAttribute` - Class-level attribute
- `McpServerPrompt` - Abstract base class representing an MCP prompt
- `AIFunctionMcpServerPrompt` - Concrete implementation wrapping `AIFunction`
- `DelegatingMcpServerPrompt` - Base class for wrapping/decorating prompts

**For Resources:**
- `McpServerResourceAttribute` - Method-level attribute with properties: `UriTemplate`, `Name`, `MimeType`
- `McpServerResourceTypeAttribute` - Class-level attribute
- `McpServerResource` - Abstract base class
- Registration: `WithResources<T>()`, `WithResourcesFromAssembly(Assembly)`

---

### 5. **Dependency Injection Integration**

**DI Container Integration - YES**, tools/prompts receive injected services:

**How it works:**

1. **Constructor Injection NOT used** - Tool and prompt classes don't use constructor injection. Instead, method parameters are injected.

2. **Parameter-based DI** - The framework inspects method parameters and automatically injects special types:

   **Special auto-bound parameters (NOT included in JSON schema):**
   - `CancellationToken` - Automatically bound; respects client cancellation
   - `IServiceProvider` - Bound from `RequestContext`
   - `McpServer` - Bound to the server instance handling the request
   - `IProgress<ProgressNotificationValue>` - For progress notifications
   - **Parameters satisfying `IServiceProviderIsService`** - Resolved from the DI container
   - **Parameters with `[FromKeyedServices(...)]`** - Resolved from keyed services

   **Regular parameters (included in JSON schema):**
   - Deserialized from `CallToolRequestParams.Arguments` dictionary as JSON
   - Use configured `JsonSerializerOptions`

3. **Services passed to tool/prompt:**
   - When registering with `WithTools<T>(instance, options)` or `WithTools<T>(options)`, you can provide an `IServiceProvider` via `McpServerToolCreateOptions.Services`
   - The same applies to prompts: `McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Services`

4. **Example with DI:**
   ```csharp
   [McpServerToolType]
   public class MyTools
   {
       [McpServerTool]
       public string MyTool(
           string userInput,  // From Arguments dict
           IServiceProvider services,  // Injected from DI
           CancellationToken ct)  // Injected for cancellation
       {
           var myService = services.GetService<IMyService>();
           return myService.Process(userInput);
       }
   }
   
   // Register with DI services
   var builder = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args);
   builder.Services.AddScoped<IMyService, MyService>();
   builder.Services.AddMcpServer(options => { /* ... */ })
       .WithTools<MyTools>();  // Services will be injected during tool invocation
   ```

**Instance Method Handling:**
- For non-static instance methods, a new instance is created for each invocation (via `CreateTarget` method that uses DI container)
- If the instance implements `IAsyncDisposable` or `IDisposable`, it's disposed after the method completes

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Registration Entry Point** | `IServiceCollection.AddMcpServer(Action<McpServerOptions>)` |
| **Returns** | `IMcpServerBuilder` (fluent API) |
| **Tool Definition** | `[McpServerToolType]` class + `[McpServerTool]` methods |
| **Prompt Definition** | `[McpServerPromptType]` class + `[McpServerPrompt]` methods |
| **Resource Definition** | `[McpServerResourceType]` class + `[McpServerResource]` methods |
| **Discovery** | Attribute-based auto-discovery via reflection |
| **Stdio Transport** | `WithStdioServerTransport()` - stdin/stdout communication |
| **DI Support** | YES - via method parameters, not constructors |
| **Special Parameters** | `CancellationToken`, `IServiceProvider`, `McpServer`, `IProgress<T>` + any service resolvable from DI |
| **Async Support** | YES - async methods supported, instance disposal after execution |

**For Prompts:**
- `McpServerPromptAttribute` - Method-level attribute
- `McpServerPromptTypeAttribute` - Class-level attribute
- `McpServerPrompt` - Abstract base class representing an MCP prompt
- `AIFunctionMcpServerPrompt` - Concrete implementation wrapping `AIFunction`
- `DelegatingMcpServerPrompt` - Base class for wrapping/decorating prompts

**For Resources:**
- `McpServerResourceAttribute` - Method-level attribute with properties: `UriTemplate`, `Name`, `MimeType`
- `McpServerResourceTypeAttribute` - Class-level attribute
- `McpServerResource` - Abstract base class
- Registration: `WithResources<T>()`, `WithResourcesFromAssembly(Assembly)`

---

### 5. **Dependency Injection Integration**

**DI Container Integration - YES**, tools/prompts receive injected services:

**How it works:**

1. **Constructor Injection NOT used** - Tool and prompt classes don't use constructor injection. Instead, method parameters are injected.

2. **Parameter-based DI** - The framework inspects method parameters and automatically injects special types:

   **Special auto-bound parameters (NOT included in JSON schema):**
   - `CancellationToken` - Automatically bound; respects client cancellation
   - `IServiceProvider` - Bound from `RequestContext`
   - `McpServer` - Bound to the server instance handling the request
   - `IProgress<ProgressNotificationValue>` - For progress notifications
   - **Parameters satisfying `IServiceProviderIsService`** - Resolved from the DI container
   - **Parameters with `[FromKeyedServices(...)]`** - Resolved from keyed services

   **Regular parameters (included in JSON schema):**
   - Deserialized from `CallToolRequestParams.Arguments` dictionary as JSON
   - Use configured `JsonSerializerOptions`

3. **Services passed to tool/prompt:**
   - When registering with `WithTools<T>(instance, options)` or `WithTools<T>(options)`, you can provide an `IServiceProvider` via `McpServerToolCreateOptions.Services`
   - The same applies to prompts: `McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Services`

4. **Example with DI:**
   ```csharp
   [McpServerToolType]
   public class MyTools
   {
       [McpServerTool]
       public string MyTool(
           string userInput,  // From Arguments dict
           IServiceProvider services,  // Injected from DI
           CancellationToken ct)  // Injected for cancellation
       {
           var myService = services.GetService<IMyService>();
           return myService.Process(userInput);
       }
   }
   
   // Register with DI services
   var builder = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args);
   builder.Services.AddScoped<IMyService, MyService>();
   builder.Services.AddMcpServer(options => { /* ... */ })
       .WithTools<MyTools>();  // Services will be injected during tool invocation
   ```

**Instance Method Handling:**
- For non-static instance methods, a new instance is created for each invocation (via `CreateTarget` method that uses DI container)
- If the instance implements `IAsyncDisposable` or `IDisposable`, it's disposed after the method completes

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Registration Entry Point** | `IServiceCollection.AddMcpServer(Action<McpServerOptions>)` |
| **Returns** | `IMcpServerBuilder` (fluent API) |
| **Tool Definition** | `[McpServerToolType]` class + `[McpServerTool]` methods |
| **Prompt Definition** | `[McpServerPromptType]` class + `[McpServerPrompt]` methods |
| **Resource Definition** | `[McpServerResourceType]` class + `[McpServerResource]` methods |
| **Discovery** | Attribute-based auto-discovery via reflection |
| **Stdio Transport** | `WithStdioServerTransport()` - stdin/stdout communication |
| **DI Support** | YES - via method parameters, not constructors |
| **Special Parameters** | `CancellationToken`, `IServiceProvider`, `McpServer`, `IProgress<T>` + any service resolvable from DI |
| **Async Support** | YES - async methods supported, instance disposal after execution |
